summary: Small build/matching fix: the Makefile now passes `-fp_contract on` in global `CFLAGS`, and the local `fmod` implementation/declaration in `sysdolphin/baselib/aobj` was made consistently single-precision by changing the header prototype from `f32 fmod(f64, f64)` to `float fmod(float, float)` and using `__fabs` instead of `fabs` inside the implementation.
what_changed: Added `-fp_contract on` to the global `CFLAGS` line in the root Makefile, alongside existing Metrowerks/Gekko flags such as `-proc gekko`, `-fp hard`, and `-O4,p`.
Changed the `fmod` implementation in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c` to compare `__fabs(b) > __fabs(a)` instead of `fabs(b) > fabs(a)`.
Changed the `fmod` declaration in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.h` from `f32 fmod(f64 x, f64 y);` to `float fmod(float x, float y);`, matching the implementation signature shown in the diff.
smart_moves: Kept the local `fmod` prototype aligned with its implementation, avoiding an ABI/type mismatch between the header and source.
Used `__fabs` in the `float fmod(float, float)` implementation, which likely steers the compiler toward the intended intrinsic/builtin behavior rather than the normal double-precision `fabs` path.
Applied `-fp_contract on` globally in the Makefile rather than patching individual source files, which is the appropriate scope when matching depends on compiler floating-point contraction behavior.
decomp_lessons: For math helpers, the header prototype must match the recovered implementation exactly; `f64` parameters in a declaration can change call-site codegen even when the source body uses `float`.
Metrowerks floating-point flags are part of the matching surface. Missing `-fp_contract on` can affect generated floating-point instructions and should be captured in the project build flags.

When replacing standard math calls during decompilation, distinguish library functions such as `fabs` from compiler builtins/intrinsics such as `__fabs` if the generated code depends on that distinction.
naming_conventions: Existing HSD baselib naming is preserved: `HSD_AObjInterpretAnim`, `HSD_AObjInvokeCallBacks`, `HSD_AObjReqAnim`, `HSD_AObjStopAnim`, `HSD_AObjLoadDesc`, `HSD_AObjRemove`, and `HSD_AObjAlloc`.
The PR keeps the local math helper named `fmod`, but makes its signature explicitly `float fmod(float x, float y)` rather than mixing `f32`/`f64` in the declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Enable floating-point contraction with `-fp_contract on` in `CFLAGS` when matching objects compiled with that behavior.
Use `__fabs` instead of `fabs` where the intended codegen is intrinsic-style absolute value handling rather than a standard library double call/conversion sequence.
Prefer exact source/header signature consistency for functions involved in float math to prevent unwanted double promotion or call ABI differences.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `-fp_contract on` or Metrowerks floating-point flags to see whether this became a standard matching requirement.
Search for other local math helper declarations that still use `f64` while their implementations use `float`.
Search for uses of `fabs` versus `__fabs` in decompiled baselib code to identify similar intrinsic matching fixes.
